The Occasion

About the Event

✦

A Rangapravesam — literally "entering the stage" — is the most sacred milestone in a classical dancer's life. It marks the formal public debut of a student who has devoted years to the rigorous, disciplined study of Kuchipudi under the guidance of a Guru.


This ceremony is as much a spiritual offering as it is a performance. The dancer presents a full-length solo recital demonstrating mastery of nritta (pure dance), nritya (expressive dance), and natya (dramatic storytelling).

Kuchipudi is one of India's oldest classical dance forms, originating in the Krishna district of Andhra Pradesh. Distinguished by its fluid, expressive movements, intricate footwork, and lyrical grace, each gesture and glance tells a story drawn from ancient texts and devotion to Lord Krishna.


Your presence on this auspicious day honors not only the dancer, but the Guru, the lineage, and the living tradition of this sacred art form.

A Celebration of Dedication

Nithya has trained under the esteemed Guru Smt. Padma Sonti through Kuchipudi Kalakshetra. This debut represents years of devotion, early mornings, and unwavering commitment to the art — celebrated with family, community, and the blessings of the divine.

Kuchipudi Kalakshetra  ·  Satish & Aparna Jonnalagedda

Kuchipudi Kalakshetra  ·  Satish & Aparna Jonnalagedda
The Teacher

Guru Smt. Padma Sonti

✦
Smt. Padma Sonti
Kuchipudi Acharya · Kuchipudi Kalakshetra

Guru Smt. Padma Sonti is one of the first to establish a Kuchipudi dance school in the DFW area. Smt. Sonti began Bharatanatyam lessons at age ten under Dr. Uma Rama Rao, continued by joining the Kuchipudi Art Academy, where she learned under the tutelage of Dr. Vempati Chinna Satyam and Smt. Shoba Naidu. She was awarded the esteemed titles of "Natya Visharada" in 1983 and "Nritya Ratna" from the Vamsee Arts Theatre in 1989. Having received the prestigious National Dance Scholarship from the Indian government, she then earned her Master's in Dance, specializing in Kuchipudi.

Smt. Padma Sonti has participated in over 300 solo and dance ballet recitals across the globe, including performances with her Gurus at the Hong Kong Festival of Asian Arts and Kuchipudi Forums across North America. She has played significant roles in classical Kuchipudi ballets such as Krishna Parijatham, Kalyana Srinivasam, and Vipranarayana. Since its inception in 1996, Kuchipudi Kalakshetra has produced many outstanding recitals and productions, including Sivananda Lahiri, Datta Darshanam, Katha Kelika, Deepavali, Hanumanth Vaibhavam, Chenchu Lakshmi, and Gajananeeyam. Over the past 30 years, Kuchipudi Kalakshetra's reputation has grown remarkably, having proudly produced 50 Rangapravesams — each one a celebration of a student's lifelong dedication to the art. The school now has its own studio in Frisco and contributes to cultural organizations including TANA, NATA, NATS, ATA, and TANTEX.

In 2007, Smt. Sonti established the Mudra Foundation, a nonprofit organization that promotes Indian classical dance. Charity performances organized by the Foundation — such as Kalamalika, Datta Darshanam, and Telugu Kala Vaibhavam — have benefited the Plano Theatre, SOS Villages, Lymphoma Research, Haiti Orphans, Vibha, the Make-A-Wish Foundation, the Scottish Rite Children's Hospital, and many more. A dedicated and sincere passion for the arts defines Guru Padma Sonti, and her pursuit of nothing less than excellence continues to inspire a genuine love of dance in all of her students.

Kuchipudi Kalakshetra  ·  Satish & Aparna Jonnalagedda
The Debutante

Nithya Jonnalagedda

✦
Nithya Jonnalagedda
Disciple of Guru Smt. Padma Sonti

Nithya was introduced to the Kuchipudi art form at the young age of seven. After moving to Frisco from Fort Worth in 2015, she learned Bharatanatyam on and off for almost a year. In 2016, she was introduced to Smt. Padma Sonti, where she began her Kuchipudi journey. Over the past several years, she has performed in various cultural events as well as charity programs organized by the Mudra Foundation and Kuchipudi Kalakshetra. She also completed her diploma in both practical and theoretical knowledge in Kuchipudi through TANA Kalasala from the prestigious Sri Padmavati Mahila Visvavidyalayam in Tirupati, India.

Outside of dance, Nithya has excelled in a variety of extracurricular endeavors. She has played golf for the past six years, has represented her high school at numerous tournaments around DFW, and served as the varsity captain of her golf team. She has also been very involved with her school's DECA chapter — advancing to the international level three years in a row and serving as co-president of one of the most successful chapters in the world. Outside of school, she was involved with her local government and served on the Mayor's Youth Council. She is also an active member of the First Tee of Greater Dallas, where she participated in the National Leadership Summit throughout her high school career. In her free time, Nithya enjoys staying active and spending quality time with friends and family.

This fall, Nithya will be attending the University of Texas at Austin, where she will be majoring in Business at the McCombs School of Business. Her Rangapravesam holds a special distinction — marking the 50th Rangapravesam produced by Kuchipudi Kalakshetra, a testament to the school's enduring legacy over nearly three decades. She seeks all of your blessings and support as she takes the stage for this momentous occasion in her dance journey and beyond.
